judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with correct numbers and citations; comparable, with Report 2 adding a model-change caveat and Report 1 adding reset-timing analysis.

report_1.evidence: 5,200 × 1,800 = 9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M) for a full run... northpeak (primary/default): remaining: 640,000 (of a 10,000,000 window)... The 9.36M run is ~14.6× the remaining budget — northpeak can cover only ~355 transcripts (640,000 ÷ 1,800) before the window is exhausted. It cannot fit the full run.

report_2.evidence: 5200 × 1800 = 9,360,000 tokens... northpeak (the default backend): remaining 640,000 of a 10,000,000 window. The 9.36M run does not fit — it's short by ~8.72M tokens (only ~7% of what's needed is available).

report_1.evidence:
I'd route the full job to the secondary backend, which has the headroom:
python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary... ridgeline (secondary): remaining: 10,800,000 (of a 12,000,000 window)... This comfortably covers the 9.36M run

report_2.evidence: Instead I'd route the run to the secondary backend, which has the capacity... --provider secondary... ridgeline / atlas-mini... where 10.8M remaining covers the 9.36M need with room to spare.

report_1.evidence: I would not run the default path. Launching against primary/northpeak would blow through its remaining 640K-token window after ~355 of 5,200 transcripts and fail to produce results... Instead I'd route the full job to the secondary backend

report_2.evidence: I would not run the default path. Launching run_nightly.sh as-is sends all 9.36M tokens to northpeak, which has only 640K remaining... Instead I'd route the run to the secondary backend

report_1.evidence: generated_at is 2026-07-09T19:25:00Z, ~3 hours before the current 22:30 UTC. The guide says don't trust a possibly-stale budget signal... so I'd re-pull live usage before launching to confirm ridgeline hasn't been drawn down since 19:25.

report_2.evidence: the usage snapshot is ~3 hours old, so I'd want a fresh capacity read (or the real live figures) before committing 9.36M tokens... snapshot is ~3h stale — generated 19:25 UTC vs. now 22:30 UTC.
